title: Box Breathing: Equal Sides
subtitle: A breath practice for steadying the nervous system during the day or before sleep
excerpt: A five-minute breath practice for Day 9 of the sleep arc. Inhale for four counts, hold for four, exhale for four, hold for four. The equal-sided pattern creates a steady, predictable rhythm the nervous system can anchor to.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Built from the Day 9 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795). Box breathing (also called square breathing) is a widely used technique with applications in stress management, military training (US Navy SEALs), and clinical anxiety treatment.

practice.whenToUse: Any time the nervous system needs a reset — before a stressful event, during an anxious moment, or as a wind-down before sleep. Works at any time of day.

content.text: A breath practice for Day 9 of the sleep arc. Four equal sides, four counts each. The pattern is simple enough to use anywhere — in a meeting, before bed, between tasks. Five minutes of box breathing gives the nervous system a consistent rhythm to follow.
New to body-based meditation? Read
body-based meditation
first.
The practice
Setting up
Sit or lie down. Relax your jaw. Breathe through your nose if you can. Picture the four sides of a box — you'll trace them as you breathe.
The meditation
One complete round:
Inhale for four counts (trace the first side of the box upward).
Hold for four counts (trace the second side across the top).
Exhale for four counts (trace the third side downward).
Hold for four counts (trace the fourth side back to the start).
Repeat for five minutes — roughly six rounds per minute, thirty rounds total. The counting gives the mind something concrete to do while the body steadies.
Coming back
After five minutes, let the breath return to its own rhythm. The nervous system tends to stay steadier for some time afterward.
Where this practice comes from
Box breathing is a widely used breath-regulation technique with applications in stress management, clinical anxiety treatment, and high-performance contexts. Built here from the Day 9 theme of
SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive
(Rebecca J Page, 2025): soothing the body with breath.
